name: Loading Skeleton
description: Placeholder content shown while data is loading. Skeleton rows match the shape and layout of actual content, providing a smooth visual experience while reducing perceived wait time. Used in tables, card lists, and other data-driven sections.
whenToUse: While fetching data from an API
During initial page load with async data
When refreshing or filtering a large dataset
To indicate that content is loading without a spinner

components.name: GoabSkeleton
components.purpose: Pre-built skeleton for common types (header, text, card)
components.note: Use for simple skeletons; custom approach for tables

customSkeletonApproach.description: For tables, create custom skeleton rows that match cell content shapes

styling.borderRadius: 4px for text, 12px for badges, 50% for avatars

example.description: Table skeleton rows matching actual data row structure

variants.name: Table skeleton
variants.description: Multiple rows matching table structure, as shown in example

variants.name: Card skeleton
variants.description: Use GoabSkeleton type='card' for card-based layouts

variants.name: With pulse animation
variants.description: Add CSS animation for subtle pulsing effect

considerations: Match skeleton shapes to actual content (badges = rounded, text = rectangular)
Vary widths slightly for natural appearance
10 rows is typical for table skeletons
Consider using GoabSkeleton component for simple cases
Skeleton should span the same columns as actual content
Keep skeleton visible for minimum ~200ms to avoid flash
